name: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN)
description: Income support of Rs 6,000 per year, paid in three equal installments, directly to the bank accounts of eligible landholding farmer families.
eligibility: Small and marginal landholding farmer families that own cultivable land. Excludes institutional landholders, income-tax payers, serving/retired government employees, and professionals like doctors and lawyers.
benefits: Rs 6,000 per year in 3 installments via Direct Benefit Transfer.

name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Gramin (PMAY-G)
description: Financial assistance to build a pucca house for rural households who are homeless or living in kutcha/dilapidated houses.
eligibility: Rural households that are houseless or living in zero/one/two-room kutcha houses, identified through SECC data and Awaas+ survey, and not already owning a pucca house.
benefits: Rs 1.20 lakh in plain areas and Rs 1.30 lakh in hilly/difficult areas, plus MGNREGA labour and a toilet under SBM.

name: Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY)
description: A small-savings scheme for the girl child offering a high fixed interest rate and tax benefits, to encourage parents to save for a daughter's education and marriage.
eligibility: Parents or legal guardians of a girl child below 10 years of age. One account per girl child, up to two girl children per family.
benefits: High government-backed interest, tax deduction under Section 80C, and tax-free maturity proceeds.

name: Pradhan Mantri Mudra Yojana (PMMY)
description: Collateral-free micro-loans to non-corporate, non-farm small and micro enterprises to start or grow a business.
eligibility: Any Indian citizen with a viable non-farm business plan for manufacturing, trading, services, or allied agriculture activities, needing credit up to Rs 10 lakh. Loans are categorised as Shishu, Kishore, and Tarun.
benefits: Collateral-free loans up to Rs 10 lakh through banks, NBFCs, and MFIs.

name: Ayushman Bharat - PM Jan Arogya Yojana (PM-JAY)
description: Health insurance cover of Rs 5 lakh per family per year for secondary and tertiary hospitalisation, for poor and vulnerable families.
eligibility: Poor and vulnerable families identified through the SECC 2011 deprivation and occupational criteria (rural and urban). Senior citizens aged 70+ are also covered irrespective of income.
benefits: Cashless hospitalisation cover of Rs 5 lakh per family per year at empanelled public and private hospitals.

name: Post-Matric Scholarship (SC/ST/OBC/Minority)
description: Scholarships to students from SC/ST/OBC/Minority communities pursuing post-matriculation (Class 11 and above) courses, to reduce dropout.
eligibility: Students belonging to notified SC/ST/OBC/Minority categories, studying in Class 11 or above, whose family income is below the scheme's ceiling (varies by category, typically Rs 2.5 lakh per year).
benefits: Maintenance allowance, reimbursement of course fees, and other allowances credited via DBT.

name: Stand-Up India
description: Bank loans between Rs 10 lakh and Rs 1 crore to Scheduled Caste, Scheduled Tribe, and women entrepreneurs for setting up a greenfield enterprise.
eligibility: SC/ST and/or women entrepreneurs above 18 years, setting up a new (greenfield) manufacturing, services, trading, or allied-agriculture enterprise. For non-individual enterprises, at least 51% stake must be held by SC/ST or a woman.
benefits: Composite loan (term + working capital) of Rs 10 lakh to Rs 1 crore per borrower.

name: PM Street Vendor's AtmaNirbhar Nidhi (PM SVANidhi)
description: Collateral-free working-capital loans for urban street vendors to resume and grow their businesses, with an interest subsidy for timely repayment.
eligibility: Urban street vendors who were vending on or before 24 March 2020 and hold a Certificate of Vending or are identified by the Urban Local Body / Town Vending Committee.
benefits: Working-capital loan starting at Rs 10,000, enhanced to Rs 20,000 and Rs 50,000 on timely repayment, with 7% interest subsidy and cashback on digital transactions.

name: Atal Pension Yojana (APY)
description: A guaranteed monthly pension scheme aimed at workers in the unorganised sector, funded by the subscriber's own contributions.
eligibility: Any Indian citizen aged 18 to 40 years with a savings bank account. Not applicable to income-tax payers (from Oct 2022).
benefits: Guaranteed monthly pension of Rs 1,000 to Rs 5,000 from age 60, based on contributions.

name: Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY)
description: Provides free LPG (cooking gas) connections to women from poor households to reduce dependence on polluting cooking fuels.
eligibility: Adult women belonging to poor households (BPL, SC/ST, PMAY beneficiaries, Antyodaya, forest dwellers, and other listed categories) who do not already have an LPG connection in the household.
benefits: Free LPG connection with a deposit-free cylinder and first refill/stove support.

name: Kisan Credit Card (KCC)
description: Provides farmers with timely and affordable short-term credit for cultivation and other agricultural needs at a subsidised interest rate.
eligibility: Farmers who are individual or joint cultivators, tenant farmers, oral lessees, sharecroppers, and members of Self Help Groups or Joint Liability Groups engaged in agriculture, animal husbandry, or fisheries.
benefits: Short-term crop loans at subsidised interest (as low as 4% per year with prompt repayment), plus a flexible credit limit.

name: Pradhan Mantri Matru Vandana Yojana (PMMVY)
description: A maternity benefit programme providing partial wage compensation to pregnant and lactating women for their first living child.
eligibility: Pregnant and lactating women aged 19 or above for the first living child. The second child is covered only if it is a girl child.
benefits: Cash incentive of Rs 5,000 for the first child (in installments) and Rs 6,000 for a second girl child, via DBT.

name: Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY)
description: Free short-term skill training and certification for youth to improve employability, including recognition of prior learning.
eligibility: Indian youth, typically 15-45 years, who are unemployed or school/college dropouts and want industry-relevant skill training. No fee for the candidate.
benefits: Free training, a government-recognised certificate, and placement assistance.

name: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS)
ministry: Department for Promotion of Industry & Internal Trade (DPIIT)
description: Financial assistance to early-stage startups for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials, and market entry.
eligibility: DPIIT-recognised startups incorporated not more than 2 years ago at the time of application, with a business idea using technology, not having received more than Rs 10 lakh of other government funding.
benefits: Grants up to Rs 20 lakh for validation/prototype and up to Rs 50 lakh for market entry via convertible debentures/debt.

name: National Social Assistance Programme (Old Age Pension - IGNOAPS)
description: Monthly pension to elderly persons from below-poverty-line households under the Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme.
eligibility: Persons aged 60 years or above belonging to a below-poverty-line (BPL) household. Pension amount increases after age 80.
benefits: Monthly old-age pension credited via DBT (amount varies by age and state top-up).

name: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY)
description: A low-premium life insurance scheme offering life cover for one year, renewable annually, linked to a bank account.
eligibility: Individuals aged 18 to 50 years holding a savings bank account who give consent to auto-debit the premium.
benefits: Life cover of Rs 2 lakh for a premium of about Rs 436 per year.

name: Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY)
description: A very low-premium accidental death and disability insurance scheme linked to a bank account.
eligibility: Individuals aged 18 to 70 years holding a savings bank account who give consent to auto-debit the premium.
benefits: Accidental death/full disability cover of Rs 2 lakh for a premium of about Rs 20 per year.

name: PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana (Rooftop Solar)
description: Subsidy for installing rooftop solar panels on homes to reduce electricity bills and promote clean energy.
eligibility: Indian households that own a house with a suitable rooftop and a valid electricity connection, and have not already availed a rooftop solar subsidy.
benefits: Central financial assistance (subsidy) covering a substantial part of the rooftop solar system cost, plus free electricity up to 300 units/month.

name: Credit Guarantee Fund for Micro & Small Enterprises (CGTMSE)
description: Provides a credit guarantee to banks so that micro and small enterprises can get collateral-free loans.
eligibility: New and existing micro and small enterprises (manufacturing or service) seeking working capital or term loans, sanctioned without third-party collateral or guarantee.
benefits: Guarantee cover on collateral-free credit up to Rs 5 crore, making bank loans easier to obtain for small businesses.

name: Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Grameen Kaushalya Yojana (DDU-GKY)
description: Skill training and assured placement for rural poor youth to enable wage employment.
eligibility: Rural youth aged 15 to 35 years from poor families (with relaxations up to 45 for women and other special groups).
benefits: Free residential/non-residential skill training with certification and guaranteed placement support.
